worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Empirical token-count data showing Opus 4.7 costs 1.46x more text tokens and up to 3x more for images than 4.6 — directly actionable for anyone budgeting agentic Claude workloads.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Critical correction to the above: the 3x image cost only applies to high-res images, not small ones — narrows the real-world cost risk considerably.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Field report of Opus 4.7 confidently sticking to wrong answers until users rolled back to 4.6, which matters for anyone deploying it in production agentic tasks.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Mollick's argument that OpenAI's early publication of o1 handed rivals the reasoning paradigm is a sharp strategic lens on open-vs-closed model release decisions.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Manifest is an open-source model router claiming 70% cost reduction by dynamically routing requests to cheaper models — directly relevant to AI product cost strategy.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Reverse-engineered visual map of Claude Code's 512k-line codebase (agent loop, orchestration, hidden features) is the most substantive Claude Code internals resource surfaced this cycle.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: The '30-day roadmap = NGMI' take has 87k views and captures the real product cadence pressure indie devs face against accelerating model releases.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Grok Build + Grok Computer as a unified desktop app is the clearest competitive threat to Codex/Claude Code's desktop agentic coding position yet.

worthClicking.author: @kloss_xyz
worthClicking.why: Concrete walkthrough of building 6 products in under 2 hours with Codex — iOS app, web app, deck, video, landing page, X automation — useful distribution-angle framing for indie devs.

voices.why: Doing actual empirical token-cost testing on Opus 4.7 and publishing corrections — the most reliable signal on Claude pricing in the feed.
Sharp takes on agent-native product strategy and distribution with real engagement; the 30-day roadmap post alone got 87k views.
Brings academic rigor to AI strategy narratives; the OpenAI reasoner history thread is the kind of context useful for product positioning.

examples: @aakashgupta posting wall-to-wall viral trivia (tennis longevity, NASA splashdowns, Nolan budgets) with zero AI relevance
@RoundtableSpace crypto/trading bot hype (Polymarket bots, $34-to-$21k weather trading, AAVE exploit)
newsletter self-promotion spam from multiple handles (4+ identical 'subscribe to my newsletter' posts from @aakashgupta)
